skill.name: web-research-assistant
skill.description: AI-powered web research assistant that leverages BrowserAct API to supplement restricted web access by searching the internet for additional information. Designed for OpenClaw and Claude Code.
skill.summary: Search the web and generate structured research reports using the BrowserAct MCP API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 43 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The skill is a legitimate CLI tool that calls the documented BrowserAct MCP API using environment credentials and optionally writes reports to user-specified files. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was detected.

content.user_title: Search the web and compile research reports
content.value_statement: AI assistants often lack direct web access or hit restricted sites. This skill uses the BrowserAct MCP API to run live searches, extract key data, and produce structured markdown or JSON research reports.
actual_capabilities: Execute live web searches via BrowserAct MCP API
Filter results by engine, content type, and time range
Extract statistics, snippets, and source URLs
Save reports to a user-specified file path
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct MCP token in the environment
Search quality depends on the BrowserAct API availability
Does not perform deep page content scraping beyond snippets
Cannot bypass paywalled content on target websites

use_cases.title: Competitor intelligence gathering
use_cases.description: Search for competitor product launches, pricing changes, and market positioning to inform business strategy.

use_cases.title: Academic and news research
use_cases.description: Find recent articles, studies, and news on a topic with time-range filtering and source citations.

use_cases.title: Technical troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Look up current error messages, library documentation, and community discussions when cached knowledge is outdated.

prompt_templates.title: Basic web search
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the latest trends in renewable energy and summarize the top findings.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted competitor search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for news about Acme Corp from the past month. Limit to 15 results and focus on articles. Save the report to acme_news.md.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-source data extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a deep research query on 'quantum computing breakthroughs 2025' using DuckDuckGo, retrieve 30 results, and output a JSON file with full sources and snippets.

prompt_templates.title: Structured report generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the global semiconductor market. Generate a markdown report with an executive summary, key statistics, data sources, and recommendations. Use the past_year time range.

output_examples.input: Research AI agent benchmarks from the past month
output_examples.output: A markdown report titled 'Web Research Report' with an executive summary stating the number of sources found, a list of key findings, extracted statistics, up to ten cited sources with titles and URLs, and a recommendations section.

output_examples.input: Search for 'electric vehicle sales 2025' and export as JSON
output_examples.output: A JSON object containing the original query, raw search results from the BrowserAct API, and an extracted object with total_results, key_facts, statistics, sources, and a summary string.

best_practices: Provide specific search queries to improve result relevance and reduce noise.
Set the time_range parameter to 'past_month' or 'past_year' when current data matters.
Always cross-verify key statistics and claims against multiple sources before making decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on a single search result as the sole source of truth.
Avoid overly broad queries that return irrelevant or low-quality results.
Do not share your BrowserAct MCP token in prompts or version control.

faq.question: What API credentials does this skill need?
faq.answer: The skill requires the BROWSERACT_MCP_TOKEN environment variable. Optionally, BROWSERACT_API_KEY may be used depending on your BrowserAct plan.

faq.question: Which AI tools are compatible with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It also works with OpenClaw and similar MCP-compatible assistants.

faq.question: Can I save the research report to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --output or -o flag followed by a file path. The skill supports both markdown and JSON output formats.

faq.question: What search engines are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo via the BrowserAct MCP API.

faq.question: Why does the skill need network access?
faq.answer: Network access is required to call the BrowserAct MCP API and perform live web searches on your behalf.

faq.question: Is my search data shared with third parties?
faq.answer: Search queries are sent to the BrowserAct MCP service. Review BrowserAct's privacy policy for details on data handling and retention.
